basis: Swiss Ephemeris 2.10, 1900-2099, apparent geocentric ecliptic longitude (true equinox of date)

bodies.name: Moon (precise tier)
bodies.note: JPL DE423 fit (2010); DE423 vs DE440 is <0.1″ over this span

bodies.name: Moon (embedded series)
bodies.note: 60-term ELP abridged

bodies.name: Uranus
bodies.note: series truncation; complete VSOP87 holds ≤1″

bodies.name: Neptune
bodies.note: series truncation; complete VSOP87 holds ≤1″

bodies.name: Pluto
bodies.note: series valid 1885-2099

bodies.name: Chiron
bodies.note: JPL Horizons fit, 1850-2150

bodies.name: True node
bodies.note: vs full JPL DE431 files; vs Swiss Ephemeris's BUILT-IN (Moshier) ephemeris expect up to ~1′ (measured: 50″ max, 8″ median, 300 epochs) — that is the built-in lunar theory's own node error, not ours

bodies.name: Mean Lilith
bodies.note: mean lunar apogee on the inclined orbit; latitude ≤0.1″

bodies.name: Sidereal longitudes
bodies.note: ayanamsa model vs SE ≤0.005″ (Vondrák 2011, same model both sides); sidereal longitudes inherit each body's tropical bound

bodies.name: RA / Dec
bodies.note: rotation is exact; bound tracks each body's ecliptic accuracy (Moon worst)

bodies.name: Topocentric Moon
bodies.note: parallax model adds ≤0.1″ over the geocentric bound

bodies.name: House cusps, 8 new systems
bodies.note: Koch, Regiomontanus, Campanus, Alcabitius, Morinus, Meridian, Polich-Page, Vehlow: exact vs swe_houses_armc (200 polar-inclusive cases each)

bodies.name: Vertex / east point
bodies.note: exact vs swe_houses_armc

bodies.name: Magnitudes
bodies.note: Mallama 2018; Moon (Allen law) valid to phase angle 140°

bodies.name: Rise/set/meridian transit
bodies.note: vs swe_rise_trans, 48 polar-inclusive cases per body; Moon bound tracks its position accuracy

bodies.name: Crossings / lunar phases
bodies.note: vs swe_solcross/swe_mooncross and elongation root-finds

bodies.name: Stations
bodies.note: ill-conditioned by nature: speed-zero slope ~0.01°/day² turns sub-arcsecond model differences into minutes

bodies.name: True Lilith (osc. apogee)
bodies.note: hypersensitive to the lunar theory (~1/e amplification); SE's own Moshier-vs-DE difference dominates. 'True Lilith' values disagree across software at this scale

bodies.name: Ceres, Pallas, Juno, Vesta, Pholus
bodies.note: JPL Horizons fits 1850-2150 (residual <5e-6 AU); same geocentric pipeline as Chiron. No independent SE oracle here: SE's asteroid files are unavailable in Moshier mode

bodies.name: Uranian bodies (Cupido…Poseidon)
bodies.note: Hamburg-school constant-element Kepler orbits, elements fitted to SE 2.10's built-in definitions (fit_uranian.py prints per-body figures; Zeus is fit-noise-limited at ~3″ heliocentric). Uranian practice works in arcminutes

bodies.name: Fixed stars (318-star catalog)
bodies.note: HYG-derived catalog (ICRS J2000 + proper motions, full 3D space motion, Vondrák 2011 precession); vs swe_fixstar fed the same rows

bodies.name: Star-anchored ayanamsas
bodies.note: galcent_0sag and true_citra computed from the apparent star; bound tracks the fixed-star chain (≤0.3″) plus the body's tropical accuracy

bodies.name: Gauquelin sectors
bodies.note: rise/set of disc center with refraction (SE method 3): exact to the rise/set bound

bodies.name: Eclipses (solar + lunar)
bodies.note: times of maximum vs swe; types exact over 1990-2030 (92 lunar + 89 solar, zero mismatches); lunar magnitudes ≤0.0013 (Danjon parallax enlargement, recovered empirically). Contact times typically ≤15 s, minutes for grazing geometries. Global circumstances only — no ground paths

summary.label: True node
summary.bound: ≤ 1′ vs SE built-in (≤ 1″ vs JPL DE431)

v03_harness: python/validate_swiss.py regenerates every figure above the line against pyswisseph 2.10 (Moshier mode)
